@@ -132,7 +132,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","title":"Implement shell tool updates (omz, p10k, plugins, atuin, zoxide)","description":"## What Implement the 'shell' category for acfs-update: - Oh-My-Zsh: Run $ZSH/tools/upgrade.sh (non-interactive) - Powerlevel10k: git pull --ff-only in $ZSH_CUSTOM/themes/powerlevel10k - zsh-autosuggestions: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - zsh-syntax-highlighting: git pull --ff-only in plugin dir - Atuin: Use atuin self-update if available, else reinstall - Zoxide: Reinstall via official installer ## Git-Based Updates (omz, p10k, plugins) - Use git pull --ff-only to avoid merge conflicts - If fast-forward fails: warn and skip (user has local changes) - OMZ has its own upgrade script that handles prompts ## Installer-Based Updates (Atuin, Zoxide) These use curl|sh installers like the stack tools: 1. Atuin: Try `atuin self-update` first (if available) - If no self-update: fetch installer, verify checksum, run 2. Zoxide: Fetch installer, verify checksum against checksums.yaml, run For checksum mismatches: same flow as stack tools (warn, prompt, skip/proceed) ## Considerations - ZSH_CUSTOM defaults to $ZSH/custom if not set - DISABLE_UPDATE_PROMPT=true for OMZ non-interactive - Some users may have customized themes/plugins - respect that ## Success Criteria - [ ] All shell tools update without prompts (in --yes mode) - [ ] Git-based updates handle conflicts gracefully - [ ] Atuin/Zoxide checksum verification works - [ ] Before/after versions logged - [ ] Skips missing components","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:26:10.463809-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:18:10.575757-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented shell tool updates: omz, p10k, zsh plugins, atuin, zoxide","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-db0","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-75e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T13:27:25.018434-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","title":"[codex.8] Audit web app for incorrect codex auth guidance","description":"## Goal Check apps/web/ for any incorrect guidance about codex-cli authentication. ## Files to Check Based on grep results: - apps/web/lib/jargon.ts - apps/web/app/wizard/run-installer/page.tsx - apps/web/lib/flywheel.ts - apps/web/app/workflow/page.tsx - apps/web/app/page.tsx ## What to Look For 1. Any mention of OPENAI_API_KEY for codex 2. Incorrect auth flow descriptions 3. Wizard steps that suggest API key setup ## Updates Needed 1. Replace API key guidance with OAuth flow description 2. Update any wizard steps about codex setup 3. Ensure jargon/definitions are correct ## Lower Priority Because The web app is less critical than the installer scripts - users who hit the web app will eventually run the installer which (after fixes) will give correct guidance. ## Acceptance Criteria 1. No incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex in web app 2. Correct OAuth flow described if mentioned","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:24.840054-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:45:56.25612-05:00","close_reason":"Audited web app - no incorrect OPENAI_API_KEY references for codex found. Jargon entry describes Codex without auth details. CAAM references are about credential management (correct).","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dcy","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ua5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:36.94908-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dfc","title":"Fix wizard layout heading causing mobile E2E failures","description":"Playwright mobile projects fail because WizardLayout renders a hidden (md:block)

step title before page content. Tests use page.locator('h1').first(), which resolves to the hidden layout heading on mobile. Also results in multiple h1s per page. Fix by removing or demoting layout heading, ensuring a single visible h1 per wizard page, and update tests if needed.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"GreenDog","created_at":"2025-12-20T21:03:08.330927-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-20T21:30:35.67678-05:00","close_reason":"Already fixed on main: apps/web/app/wizard/layout.tsx no longer renders a hidden

step title; only the step indicator remains, so mobile h1 selector mismatch is resolved.","labels":["a11y","ci","e2e","web"]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:26:57.409729-05:00"} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dmff","title":"Add headless browser auth flow explanation","description":"# Task: Add Headless Browser Auth Flow Explanation ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Authentication Flow for Headless VPS (agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-3edj) ## Description When users run `claude` or `gh auth login` on the VPS, these tools try to open a browser. But there's no browser on the VPS! The tool prints a URL that must be opened on the user's laptop. This "device flow" is completely unexplained. ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/status-check/page.tsx Add explanation before the auth commands: \`\`\`tsx

Your VPS doesn't have a web browser, so authentication works a bit differently:

  1. Run the login command (like claude)
  2. The terminal will display a URL (or code)
  3. Copy that URL and paste it in your laptop's browser
  4. Complete the login in your browser
  5. Return to your terminal — it should confirm success
If you see "Opening browser..." but nothing happens, that's normal! Just copy the URL shown and open it manually.
\`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains the device flow clearly - [ ] Step-by-step numbered list - [ ] Addresses "nothing happened" confusion - [ ] Appears before the auth command list - [ ] Works well on mobile ## UI/UX Notes - Use Laptop icon to reinforce "use your laptop's browser" - This should feel like a helpful heads-up, not a warning - Consider adding a visual diagram showing the flow","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:42:00.199364-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:20.959969-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented: AlertCard with Laptop icon explaining device auth flow with 5-step process and 'Opening browser...' confusion addressed at lines 179-197"} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive unit coverage (no mocks) + E2E logging","description":"# Goal Establish real-data, no-mock unit coverage and complete E2E integration scripts with detailed, human-readable logging. # Why - Current tests are partial (installer E2E + web Playwright). No full unit coverage across core Bash libs or manifest tooling. - E2E logs exist but are not standardized or granular enough for fast diagnosis. # Success Criteria - Unit coverage exists for manifest parser/generator, selection resolver, contract/security/preflight libs with real fixtures (no mocks/fakes). - E2E scripts cover bootstrap + selection + resume flows with structured logs and artifacts. - Tests are deterministic, diffable, and runnable locally + in CI. # Notes - "No mocks/fakes" means: use real manifests, real generated scripts, real flags; avoid stubbing core behaviors. - Logging should be timestamped, sectioned, and include command echo + exit codes. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:13.75871-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:16:59.6662-05:00","close_reason":"All 6 children completed: coverage matrix, parser/generator tests (132 tests), installer lib tests (15 tests), test harness, selection E2E (26 tests), web E2E runner script.","labels":["qa","tests"]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","title":"Define coverage matrix + real fixtures catalog","description":"# Scope Create a concrete coverage matrix that maps each core component to a test type, and standardize a catalog of **real** fixtures used by unit tests. # Deliverables - Coverage matrix table (component → test type → owner → status). - Fixture catalog list (real manifest YAMLs, generated scripts, seed state files) with storage location and usage notes. # Detailed comments - Components to include: `packages/manifest` (schema/parser/generator), `scripts/lib` (install_helpers, contract, security, preflight, logging), `install.sh` selection/introspection, `tests/vm` E2E. - Fixtures must be real (valid YAMLs and generated outputs), not synthetic mocks. - Ensure fixtures are minimal but realistic; avoid brittle snapshots. # Acceptance criteria - Matrix is stored in repo (likely `docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md`). - Fixture catalog references actual files committed in repo. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.951298-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:46:31.078442-05:00","close_reason":"Added docs/tests/coverage_matrix.md and docs/tests/fixtures_catalog.md with real-file coverage matrix + fixture catalog","labels":["qa","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:21.953283-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.2","title":"Unit tests: manifest parser/generator using real fixtures","description":"# Scope Add unit tests for `packages/manifest` (schema, parser, dependency resolution, generator outputs) using real YAML fixtures. # Detailed comments - Use real `acfs.manifest.yaml` plus a curated set of small, valid fixtures (no mocks). - Cover schema validation (new fields), dependency closure, cycle detection, and deterministic module ordering. - Validate generated `manifest_index.sh` content matches fixture expectations (IDs, phases, deps, defaults). # Acceptance criteria - Tests run via `bun test` (or existing harness) with real fixtures. - Failures are actionable (clear assertions and output). ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:28.350421-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T16:58:31.870363-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T16:58:31.870363-05:00","close_reason":"Added comprehensive unit tests: schema.test.ts, parser.test.ts, generate.test.ts - 132 tests pass","labels":["manifest","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:25:28.352603-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-dvt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T16:26:03.792033-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} @@ -173,7 +173,7 @@ {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-itb","title":"[ubu.12] Add snapshot recommendation and safety warnings","description":"# Add Snapshot Recommendation and Safety Warnings ## Purpose OS upgrades are one-way operations. Users should be clearly warned and encouraged to snapshot before proceeding. ## Pre-Upgrade Warning Message Display before starting any upgrade: ```bash ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning() { local current=$(ubuntu_get_version_string) local target="$TARGET_UBUNTU_VERSION" local path=$(ubuntu_calculate_upgrade_path "$target") local hops=$(echo "$path" | wc -w) local estimated_time=$((hops * 45)) cat < ## DigitalOcean 1. Open Droplets dashboard 2. Click on your Droplet 3. Go to "Snapshots" tab 4. Click "Take Snapshot" ``` ## Confirmation for Interactive Mode For users not using --yes: ```bash ubuntu_confirm_upgrade() { if [[ "$YES_MODE" == "true" ]]; then log_info "Auto-confirmed (--yes mode)" return 0 fi ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning echo "" echo "Have you taken a snapshot? (Recommended but not required)" echo "" read -p "Proceed with Ubuntu upgrade? [y/N] " response if [[ ! "$response" =~ ^[Yy] ]]; then log_info "Upgrade cancelled by user" return 1 fi return 0 } ``` ## Non-Interactive Mode When running with --yes, still show warning but proceed: ```bash # In --yes mode, warning is informational only ubuntu_show_upgrade_warning log_warn "Proceeding automatically (--yes mode)" log_warn "If you need to abort, press Ctrl+C within 10 seconds" sleep 10 ``` ## Rollback Information Provide clear guidance that there is NO rollback: ```bash ubuntu_explain_no_rollback() { cat < {/* Optional: Top nav breadcrumb */}
← Back to Learning Hub
{/* Main content */}
{children}
); } ``` #### 2. Lesson Sidebar (`components/lesson-sidebar.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonSidebarProps { currentSlug: string; } export function LessonSidebar({ currentSlug }: LessonSidebarProps) { const [completed] = useCompletedLessons(); return (
{LESSONS.map(lesson => { const isComplete = completed.includes(lesson.id); const isCurrent = lesson.slug === currentSlug; return ( {isComplete ? "✓" : isCurrent ? "●" : "○"} {lesson.title} ); })}
); } ``` #### 3. Lesson Navigation (`components/lesson-nav.tsx`) ```typescript interface LessonNavProps { currentId: number; onMarkComplete: () => void; isComplete: boolean; } export function LessonNav({ currentId, onMarkComplete, isComplete }: LessonNavProps) { const prev = getLessonById(currentId - 1); const next = getLessonById(currentId + 1); return (
{prev ? ( ) :
} {next ? ( ) : ( )}
); } ``` ### Responsive Behavior - **Desktop**: Two-column layout with sidebar - **Tablet**: Sidebar collapses to top nav or hamburger - **Mobile**: No sidebar, just content + bottom nav ### Reading Progress Indicator (Optional Enhancement) - Show scroll progress bar at top - Auto-mark complete when user scrolls to bottom? ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Layout renders correctly on `/learn/[slug]` pages - [ ] Sidebar shows all 9 lessons with status - [ ] Current lesson highlighted in sidebar - [ ] Previous/Next navigation works correctly - [ ] "Mark Complete" button toggles completion - [ ] Last lesson shows "Continue to Workflow" instead of Next - [ ] Responsive on all screen sizes ## File Changes - Create: `apps/web/app/learn/layout.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-sidebar.tsx` - Create: `apps/web/components/lesson-nav.tsx`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:20:31.097728-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T18:26:13.512264-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in lesson-content.tsx as part of tor: desktop sidebar, lesson list with completion status, current highlight, prev/next nav, mark complete, responsive mobile nav","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-tor","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:07.727673-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-j9a","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-ori","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:24:08.278443-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} -{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-k80e","title":"Add filesystem orientation guide (where are my files?)","description":"# Task: Add Filesystem Orientation Guide ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide ## Problem Users don't know: - Where their files are located on the VPS - How to navigate the filesystem - Basic commands for moving around - What the directory structure looks like ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx or /learn/filesystem Add section: \`\`\`tsx

Finding Your Way Around

Your home folder

Everything you create lives in /home/ubuntu (or just ~).

See what's here

We installed lsd — a prettier version of ls.

Navigate into a folder

Find files fast

Pro tip: Use z (zoxide) to jump to folders you've visited before. Just type z proj to jump to your projects folder! \`\`\` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Explains home folder location (~) - [ ] Shows lsd for listing (not plain ls) - [ ] Shows cd for navigation - [ ] Shows search tools (rg, fd) - [ ] Mentions zoxide for fast jumping ## Why This Matters Users who can't navigate are stuck. Basic orientation enables independence.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:56:21.352397-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T13:56:21.352397-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-k80e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1u8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:38.466104-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} +{"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-k80e","title":"Add filesystem orientation guide (where are my files?)","description":"# Task: Add Filesystem Orientation Guide ## Parent Epic [EPIC] Daily Workflow & Getting Started Guide ## Problem Users don't know: - Where their files are located on the VPS - How to navigate the filesystem - Basic commands for moving around - What the directory structure looks like ## Implementation Details Location: apps/web/app/wizard/launch-onboarding/page.tsx or /learn/filesystem Add section: \`\`\`tsx

Basic orientation enables independence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:56:21.352397-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-22T14:27:14.454868-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-k80e","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-1u8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-22T13:57:38.466104-05:00","created_by":"jemanuel"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","title":"Update README with new features documentation","description":"# Task: Update README with new features documentation ## Context All epics need user-facing documentation in the existing README. ## Features to Document ### 1. Resume Capability - Explain that install now resumes automatically - --force-reinstall flag for fresh install - Silent resume by default (no prompts) ### 2. Pre-Flight Check - Optional `./scripts/preflight.sh` command - What it checks (disk, memory, network, apt locks) - How to interpret pass/warn/fail output ### 3. Better Error Messages - Explain structured error output format - Where to find logs - Common fixes in ERROR_PATTERNS ### 4. Checksum Recovery - Batched mismatch handling - CRITICAL vs RECOMMENDED tool behavior - Manual install for skipped tools ### 5. Enhanced Doctor - `acfs doctor --deep` flag - What functional tests check (auth, databases) - Intentional skip vs missing tool distinction ## Sections to Update (NOT add new) - Quick Install → mention resume behavior - Troubleshooting → error patterns, preflight - Doctor section → --deep flag, output format ## What NOT to Add - Contributing section (AGENTS.md: "Remove any mention of contributing/contributors") - Separate new sections for each feature (integrate into existing) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] All new features documented - [ ] Examples provided - [ ] Integrated into existing sections - [ ] No new "Contributing" section - [ ] Concise - users should READ the README, not skim it ## Files to Modify - README.md (only)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T12:51:56.158321-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:10:06.802722-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:10:06.802722-05:00","close_reason":"Documented resume capability, pre-flight check, and doctor --deep in README","comments":[{"id":6,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","author":"jemanuel","text":"Heads up: manifest-driven installer epic will add new user-facing flags () and recommend curl|bash installs pin to tags/SHA via archive bootstrap. Coordinate README edits with this.","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:08Z"},{"id":7,"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","author":"jemanuel","text":"Heads up: manifest-driven installer epic agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt will add user-facing flags (--only/--skip/--print-plan) and recommend curl|bash installs pin to tags/SHA via archive bootstrap. Coordinate README edits with this.","created_at":"2025-12-21T18:50:34Z"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.1","title":"Add Mermaid architecture diagram to README","description":"## Goal Add a GitHub-rendering Mermaid diagram to `README.md` that explains ACFS at a glance. ## Diagram requirements - Must render on github.com (```mermaid fenced block). - Show the major components and how they connect: - `apps/web` (Next.js wizard; no backend; state via URL/localStorage) - `install.sh` orchestrator - `scripts/lib/*` shared libs (logging/security/state/etc) - `acfs.manifest.yaml` (single source of truth) - `packages/manifest` generator (produces `scripts/generated/*`) - `scripts/generated/*` (module installers + `manifest_index` + doctor checks) - `acfs/` assets copied to `~/.acfs/` - `acfs doctor` (current + future generated checks) ## Acceptance criteria - `README.md` includes the diagram in an existing section (no new big section sprawl). - Diagram matches the real flow: manifest → generator → generated scripts, and `install.sh` sources libs + generated scripts. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:45.373642-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:11:13.68329-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:11:13.68329-05:00","close_reason":"Already implemented - comprehensive Mermaid architecture diagram exists at README.md lines 126-190 (One-Page System Data Flow) covering all requested components: apps/web, install.sh, scripts/lib/*, acfs.manifest.yaml, packages/manifest, scripts/generated/*, acfs/, and acfs doctor","labels":["docs","readme","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.1","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:45.379171-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]} {"id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.2","title":"Document Vault secrets posture (install + usage)","description":"## Goal Document that ACFS includes HashiCorp Vault for secrets management, and explain the intended “default beginner” posture vs advanced usage. ## Why - Users need a consistent story for secrets (agent keys, DB creds, API tokens). - Vault is listed as part of the stack; README should make it explicit and non-confusing. ## Scope - README only (integrate into existing sections). - Clarify whether Vault is installed by default vs opt-in. - Link the idea of “secrets management” to the broader ACFS goals (reproducible infra + agent workflow). ## Acceptance criteria - README explicitly mentions Vault as the recommended secrets tool. - README clarifies installation default (enabled-by-default vs opt-in) and how to verify it’s present. - README avoids implying external contributions or PR workflow. ","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:53.147283-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:58.112745-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T15:13:58.112745-05:00","close_reason":"Already documented in README Cloud/DB section: Vault installed by default, --skip-vault to skip, CLI only (no server config)","labels":["docs","readme","secrets"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:52:53.149053-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-kgf.2","depends_on_id":"agentic_coding_flywheel_setup-mjt.3.4","type":"related","created_at":"2025-12-21T14:53:43.160539-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
